# Test fixtures

Small, committed sample data so tests, the offline report renderer, and the pipeline
`-stub` run all work with **no downloads and no heavy tools**.

| File | What it is | Used by |
|---|---|---|
| `sample.markdup.metrics` | A minimal Picard/GATK MarkDuplicates metrics file | `test_build_metrics.py`, manual `build_metrics.py` runs |
| `sample.happy.summary.csv` | A minimal `hap.py` summary (SNP + INDEL rows) | `test_build_metrics.py` |
| `HG002_chr20.metrics.json` | A complete example pipeline output record | `infer.py --offline` demo |
| `../../pipeline/assets/testdata/tiny_ref.fa` | ~600 bp toy reference | `-profile test -stub` |
| `../../pipeline/assets/testdata/tiny_truth.vcf.gz` | Minimal truth VCF | `-profile test -stub` |
| `../../pipeline/assets/testdata/tiny_truth.bed` | Minimal high-confidence BED | `-profile test -stub` |
| `../../pipeline/assets/testdata/HG002_chr20_R{1,2}.fastq.gz` | ~50 toy paired reads | `-profile test -stub` |

The tiny genomic files are **placeholders for the stub run only** — they let the pipeline's
structure/DAG be validated without real data. For a real run, `scripts/fetch_testdata.sh`
stages the actual GIAB HG002 chr20 data. The genomic placeholders are regenerated by
`scripts/make_tiny_testdata.py`.

## Quick demo (no dependencies beyond Python)

```bash
python3 ai-report/infer.py --metrics tests/fixtures/HG002_chr20.metrics.json --offline
```
